comments:
Hydrogen Element — Pure Data Description of UI

This module defines the core Element type that describes UI as pure data.
Elements are not tied to any rendering target — they can be interpreted to:

- Halogen HTML (reactive web components)
- Direct DOM (browser FFI)
- Static HTML strings (SSG)
- Canvas 2D (motion graphics)
- WebGL (3D rendering)
- Native views (React Native, Flutter)

## Design Principles

Following libevring's pattern: **separate what from how**.

```
Element × Event → Element × [Effect]
```

Elements are pure, testable, serializable. Targets execute against reality.

## Usage

```purescript
import Hydrogen.Render.Element as E

myButton :: forall msg. msg -> String -> Element msg
myButton onClick label =
  E.element "button"
    [ E.onClick onClick
    , E.class_ "btn btn-primary"
    ]
    [ E.text label ]
```

declarations.comments:
Hydrogen Element — pure data description of a UI node

The `msg` type parameter represents the message type that event handlers
can produce. This follows the Elm architecture pattern.
declarations.title: Element

declarations.comments: Attribute on an element — either a property, attribute, or event handler
declarations.title: Attribute

declarations.comments: Namespace for elements (HTML vs SVG vs MathML)
declarations.title: Namespace

declarations.comments: Create an element with a tag name, attributes, and children
declarations.title: element

declarations.comments: Create a keyed element for efficient list diffing
declarations.title: keyed

declarations.comments: Create a lazy element that defers rendering
declarations.title: lazy

declarations.comments: Meta element (void element, typically in head)
declarations.title: meta_

declarations.comments: Link element (void element, typically in head)
declarations.title: link_

declarations.comments: Create a namespaced attribute (e.g., xlink:href for SVG)
declarations.title: attrNS

declarations.comments:
Create a boolean DOM property

Boolean properties render as present (attribute name only) when true,
and are omitted entirely when false.
declarations.title: propBool

declarations.comments: Map over the message type of an element
declarations.title: mapMsg

declarations.comments: Prepend a child to an element
declarations.title: prependChild

declarations.comments: Append a child to an element
declarations.title: appendChild

declarations.comments: Add an attribute to an element
declarations.title: withAttribute

declarations.comments: Add multiple attributes to an element
declarations.title: withAttributes

declarations.comments:
Force evaluation of a lazy element
Returns the element unchanged if not lazy
declarations.title: forceLazy

declarations.comments: Recursively force all lazy elements in a tree
declarations.title: forceAllLazy

declarations.comments: Get the number of direct children of an element
declarations.title: childCount

declarations.comments: Get the first child of an element if it exists
declarations.title: firstChild

declarations.comments: Get the last child of an element if it exists
declarations.title: lastChild

declarations.comments: Check if an element has no children
declarations.title: isEmpty
